chosen: LoRA reduces training cost by inserting low-rank trainable matrices into attention or projection layers while freezing the original model weights.
rejected: LoRA is just a vague training trick and does not provide any practical efficiency benefit.

chosen: DPO directly optimizes a policy to prefer chosen responses over rejected ones using paired preference data, avoiding a separate reward model in the basic setup.
rejected: DPO only memorizes the longer answer and does not use preference signals.
